description: GST rate bands as introduced on 1 July 2017 — the launch date of the Goods and Services Tax in India
source: CGST Act 2017; CBIC Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) and companion state notifications; GST Council recommendations from the 14th GST Council Meeting
notes: These are the rate bands at launch. Individual HSN-to-rate assignments change frequently at GST Council meetings; see hsn/ directory for current assignments. Rate values are percentages. CGST + SGST split is always 50/50 of the total rate (e.g., 18% = 9% CGST + 9% SGST) for intra-state supply. For inter-state supply, the full rate is collected as IGST. The 0.25% rate applies specifically to rough diamonds and certain precious stones. The 3% rate applies to gold, silver, diamonds, and certain precious metals.

rates.applicability: Essential items: fresh food, basic services, education, healthcare, and other exempted supplies

rates.description: Special rate for rough precious stones
rates.applicability: Rough diamonds and specified precious stones

rates.description: Gold, silver, and specified precious metals
rates.applicability: Gold, silver, platinum, imitation jewellery (effective rate on precious metals supply)
Household necessities, packaged food, footwear under ₹1000, apparel under ₹1000, domestic LPG, and other items in the 5% schedule
Processed food, computers, mobile phones (initial), business class air travel, and other items in the 12% schedule. Subject to rationalization discussions.
Most services, industrial intermediates, consumer electronics, restaurants (non-AC and AC differ, see applicability notes), and the default rate for items not classified elsewhere

Luxury and sin goods: tobacco products, aerated beverages, luxury automobiles, pan masala, and other items in the 28% schedule. Compensation cess applies in addition to the 28% for many items — see cess/ dataset.
